programs.name: Big Sky Waiver Program
programs.what_it_does: Montana's Big Sky Waiver is a Medicaid 1915(c) HCBS waiver that provides long-term services and supports to help elderly or disabled individuals avoid nursing home placement and live independently in their home, a relative's home, or community settings like assisted living.[1][2] Benefits include adult day care, personal care, homemaker services, skilled nursing, home modifications, companion services, meal delivery, and respite care, with individualized service plans.[1][2]
programs.who_its_for: Montana residents aged 65+ or physically disabled adults under 65 at risk of institutionalization; 2025 income limit $967/month (individual), asset limit $2,000.[1][2]
programs.whats_unique: State-specific Medicaid waiver unique to Montana (not available in other states), with capped slots (2,783 max, including reserves for assisted living and nursing home transitions via Money Follows the Person); not an entitlement, may have waitlists.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by individualized plan; covers services like skilled nursing and home mods up to program limits (e.g., 28 slots for assisted living at risk of institutionalization).[1][2]

programs.name: Community First Choice Services (CFCS) / Personal Care Services (PCS)
programs.what_it_does: Provides in-home personal assistance for daily activities like bathing, dressing, toileting, ambulation, and meal preparation to support independent living. Additional services through CFCS include personal emergency response systems.[2][4][6]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors and disabled persons meeting Medicaid financial and functional eligibility; entitlement program without waiting list.[2]
programs.benefit_value: Hands-on assistance as needed; no waitlist for eligible applicants.[2][4]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Not explicitly detailed in Montana-specific sources; federal PACE provides comprehensive medical and social services to enable frail seniors to live in the community. Availability in Montana not confirmed in results.
programs.who_its_for: Frail seniors 55+ certified for nursing home level care but able to live safely in community.

programs.name: Medicaid for the Aged, Blind, and Disabled (ABD)
programs.what_it_does: Provides Medicaid coverage for long-term care and other health services for seniors and disabled individuals. Includes estate recovery for costs from age 55+ (excludes expansion enrollees).[5]
programs.who_its_for: Aged, blind, or disabled Medicare beneficiaries with limited income/resources per Montana guidelines.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Full Medicaid benefits including LTSS; estate recovery applies post-55.[5]

programs.name: Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), Qualifying Individual (QI)
programs.what_it_does: Montana Medicare Savings Programs pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for eligible low-income seniors. Administered under state Medicaid guidelines for ABD.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare beneficiaries with limited income/resources meeting Montana ABD financial criteria.[5]

programs.name: SNAP (Food Stamps)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly food assistance benefits to low-income seniors. Seniors 60+ have simplified application, medical expense deductions over $35/month, and access to senior food banks.[1][9]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income individuals/households, especially seniors 60+ with income under $1,580/month (individual).[1]
programs.benefit_value: Up to $291/month maximum; restaurant meals in some areas.[1]

programs.name: Montana Energy Assistance (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers grants to help low-income households, including seniors, pay heating costs. Application open October–March.[1]

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides home weatherization services to improve energy efficiency for low-income households. Available in Montana for seniors.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Free or low-cost home improvements (insulation, etc.).[1]

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free counseling on Medicare options through Montana Area Agencies on Aging (AAAs). Assists with Medicare Savings Programs and long-term care planning.[4][5]
programs.who_its_for: Montana Medicare beneficiaries seeking guidance on coverage and assistance.[5]

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Not detailed by specific Montana name; delivers meals to homebound seniors (federal/state-funded via AAAs). Supports nutrition for those unable to prepare food.
programs.benefit_value: N/A; typically 5-14 meals/week; limited state-specific details.

programs.name: Respite Care (via Big Sky Waiver)
programs.what_it_does: Provides temporary relief for caregivers of seniors/disabled through paid respite services. Included in Big Sky Waiver to support family caregivers.[2][4]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors/disabled enrolled in Big Sky Waiver and their caregivers.[2]

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Not explicitly named in Montana results; federal program offers part-time paid training jobs for low-income seniors 55+. Focuses on employment and community service.
programs.benefit_value: N/A; minimum wage training positions; limited MT details.

programs.name: Legal Services for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Not specified by Montana name; elder law attorneys and AAAs assist with legal issues like Medicaid planning. NAELA provides local elder law attorney search.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors needing legal help with benefits/LTC.[5]
programs.benefit_value: Varies; free/low-cost via AAAs or pro bono.[5]

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Not explicitly detailed; advocates for residents in long-term care facilities (administered via AAAs/SLTC). Resolves complaints and protects rights.
